theory.canonical_statement: Lemniscatic Bifurcation Point of Systems At Scale.
theory.technical_interpretation: A Trawinistic manifold is a smooth manifold $(M,g)$ equipped with a Lorentzian metric $g$ such that $\det g|_p = 0$ at the TZP and $g$ is non-degenerate elsewhere.

canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
\forall \Gamma \subset M,\;
\Gamma \sim \Lambda
\]
\[
\Lambda(0)=\Lambda(1/2)=p
\]
where $\Gamma$ is any closed loop encircling the TZP and $\Lambda$ is its lemniscatic representative.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This identifies the class of loops whose topology is sensitive to the zero point rather than ordinary ambient geometry.
This pushes the loop into the canonical figure-eight form and makes the topological progression readable instead of merely abstract.
This closes the argument by forcing both branches of the lemniscate to meet at the same singular anchor, which later drives dimensional ambiguity.

external_sources.title: TZP TRAWIN ZERO POINT
external_sources.canonical_claim: Lemniscatic Bifurcation Point of Systems At Scale.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Reflections on the Lemniscate of Bernoulli: The Forty‑Eight Faces of a Mathematical Gem) supports the mathematical structure (lemniscate self‑intersection) by showing that The article discusses the lemniscate of Bernoulli, highlighting that this curve has three self‑intersection nodes (double points) that must be permuted by any projective symmetry.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.source_title: Reflections on the Lemniscate of Bernoulli: The Forty‑Eight Faces of a Mathematical Gem
external_sources.relevance: The article discusses the lemniscate of Bernoulli, highlighting that this curve has three self‑intersection nodes (double points) that must be permuted by any projective symmetry. It shows that the lemniscate possesses a self‑intersection and is invariant under certain symmetries, supporting the idea of a lemniscatic bifurcation at the zero point.
